extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE won the 27.2km Pau time trial by 14 seconds over THOMAS Geraint, extending yellow jersey advantage on terrain favoring the GC specialist.
tactical_patterns: DE GENDT Thomas led for hours despite crashing near finish, revealing depth gaps when GC men started late.
THOMAS Geraint gained 21 seconds on DE GENDT at summit climb, showing ITT power concentration on uphill sections matters more.
BERNAL Egan conceded minute to THOMAS Geraint as teammate, confirming Ineos hierarchy firmly established despite mountain stages ahead.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian fastest on final climb segment after mediocre flat splits, proving puncheur power translates to hilly ITTs.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: won hilly ITT over pure TT specialist THOMAS, suggesting yellow jersey defensible into mountains unlike typical French challengers
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: lost to ALAPHILIPPE despite being best current-generation GT time trialist, but gained significantly on other GC rivals
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: led most of day after 200km stage win earlier, showing unusual diesel TT power for breakaway specialist
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: finished 20th conceding minute to teammate THOMAS, confirming weakness against clock eliminates him from yellow contention pre-mountains
